name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: Are you watching this?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
Friend,

I am normally a pretty even tempered person, but I am watching this GOP debate and my temperature is rising rapidly. Will they continue to support the same old trickle-down economics that rewards a wealthy few at the expense of everyone else? Will they continue to deny the reality of climate change? Will they continue their assault on the Affordable Care Act and funding for women's health? The answer is yes, yes, and yes.

We must say no to this destructive agenda. **But here's the scary thing: one of these candidates _could_ be elected President.**

You say it can't happen? That's what they said about the last governor's race in Maryland--that we would never elect a Republican governor. But you know what happened.

That's why we have to work so hard in 2016 to elect a Democrat for President. **We also have to work overtime to elect a Democratic Senate to build a firewall to prevent the ideas being promoted by these Republican candidates from ever being enacted.**

And winning the Senate begins with Maryland. We can't win the Senate if Maryland does in 2016 what it did in 2014. So we need you now.

**_[Will you give $5 or more right now to help us win and take back the Senate in 2016?](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91e9c/70cdc2ba/479c2f17/39509ec/4041909542/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/takebackseante?refcode=em080615nd&amount=5.00")_**

Thanks,

Chris

name: Ben Carson
subject: An incredible night in Cleveland
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Carson America, Inc
body:
---
| | [](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/29592061:28372826316:m:1:876315805:F97E975BD89470104DAA8CAA8EBE5962:r)
---

Peter,

What a night.

I had the absolute honor of joining nine of my fellow candidates in Cleveland last night to share my vision for America - and I must thank you for making it possible.

In my closing statement, I talked about the need to pick up the baton of freedom, because freedom is not free and we must fight for it every day.

The feedback has been incredible, and I'd like to share some of it with you now. This is from a CNN focus group of undecided Republican voters in Iowa:
Who had the best breakout performance? In Pella, Ben Carson stood out, described as "ready for prime time," "brilliant," and "someone you can really trust."

"Everything about him is impressive," said Lynne Henry. "I just get a real sense of peace and calm with this man that I have not felt from a candidate since Ronald Reagan."

By the end of the night, Henry, who went into the debate interested in both Trump and Carson, was ready to make a decision: "I have been holding onto a Ben Carson bumper sticker that got sent to me, I've been waiting to put it on my car. It's going on tonight."
Peter, last night was progress, there's no other way to put it.

All we can do now is keep our foot on the gas, and double our efforts so we can reach more voters like Lynne in Iowa.

**These are the times where I need your help, and there are two specific things you can do.**

[**First, please make a contribution so we can continue building our grassroots infrastructure, which is of vital importance.**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/29592061:28372826316:m:1:876315805:F97E975BD89470104DAA8CAA8EBE5962:r)

**And two, please send an email to your friends and family, and ask them to sign up to join us at**[**www.BenCarson.com**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/29592062:28372826316:m:1:876315805:F97E975BD89470104DAA8CAA8EBE5962:r)**.**

Thank you again for making last night possible, and together, let's continue to carry the baton of freedom.

Sincerely,

Ben Carson

name: randhpaulmd@gmail.com
subject: The first punches were thrown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Fellow Conservative:

The field is set. The first punches were thrown during the first Republican presidential debate in Cleveland.

And the American people are now "locking in."

With more debate fireworks and media SMEARS on my campaign right around the corner, I'm counting on your IMMEDIATE action.

**[Will you please click here to sign your STAND WITH RAND Supporter's Pledge right away](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/29594437:28378026825:m:1:879035888:B7F4FCA4C9C0E7B86CD1F15C8F9B9FBF:r)**?

I believe your action today can give my campaign the vital burst of post-debate momentum I need heading into the FEC's End of Quarter reporting period next month.

But it's absolutely critical you act IMMEDIATELY.

This is crunch time. The stakes are getting higher every single day. And it's anybody's race.

The national media and Big Government members of BOTH parties want more than anything to dismiss my message of liberty and limited government.

They tell us the Republican presidential nomination has already been decided - or at least narrowed down to a few "acceptable" Republicans. That those of us who believe in liberty and limited government should step aside for someone else "to get their turn."

All I can say is the race for the White House is not an electoral soap opera ruled by political bluebloods!

And I'm not just going to step aside so the Washington Machine can continue piling on our $18.3 TRILLION debt and assaulting our remaining constitutional liberties.

All those polls that show me leading Hillary Clinton in key primary states tell me the American people won't either.

I believe the American people are fed up with the status quo -- with the Washington Machine's constant spending, spying and scandals.

They're ready for a President who will FIGHT to:

*** CUT TAXES AND SPENDING. My Fair and Flat Tax plan will blow up the tax code and start over, return $2 TRILLION to the American people over ten years, grow our GDP by a whopping 10% and result in at least 1.4 million new jobs!

*** PASS TERM LIMITS to end the reign of career politicians in Washington, D.C.;

*** FORCE MEMBERS OF CONGRESS TO READ THE BILLS THEY VOTE ON, and stop their habit of ramming thousand-page bills into law at the last minute before anyone knows what's in them;

*** REPEAL ALL OF OBAMACARE to stop the soaring health insurance premium costs, policy cancellations, shuttered hospitals and poorer quality healthcare Americans are now experiencing;

*** AUDIT THE FEDERAL RESERVE to end the "print-now, ask-questions-later" policies that result in never-ending boom-bust economic cycles, corruption and cronyism;

*** PROTECT THE ENTIRE BILL OF RIGHTS. That means protecting our religious liberties, political speech, gun rights and our Fourth Amendment right to privacy from government spying.

This isn't a plan to "manage" Big Government and kick the can down the road until the entire American economy simply self-destructs.

It's a plan to END Big Government and RESTORE the freedom and prosperity that should be every American's birthright.

This is what has the media and Big Government apologists in BOTH parties scared.

It's why they're coming after me with everything they have.

It's why I've been attacked for standing up for our Constitution's Fourth Amendment.

For REFUSING to wave the white flag on ObamaCare. For demanding Washington, D.C. finally secures our border. For daring to call out pro-abortion Democrats for their extremism.

For rejecting the lie that the best way for our Republican Party to defeat Hillary Clinton is to be just like her.

In fact, _The Hill_ has reported that the Democrat National Committee has attacked me more than any other Republican candidate for President!

I have no doubt that my opponents will continue ganging up on me with everything they have during the Republican debates as well.

But if you will continue to stand with me, I believe we can face down these assaults and WIN.

That's why I need to hear from you IMMEDIATELY.

With the launch of the presidential debates, many Americans are just now "tuning in" to the race for the White House.

That's why it's so critical my campaign can show a surge of momentum now.

Your STAND WITH RAND Supporter's Pledge will let me know you have my back, which will mean the world to me in the midst of all these attacks.

body:
Fellow Conservative,

If the American people wanted fireworks in last night's Republican debate, they got them.

They also learned -- when the Governor of New Jersey tried to attack me -- I will not ever back down from defending the United States Constitution. PERIOD.

Now, with the American people "locking in" to the presidential race like never before, I must count on your IMMEDIATE support.

**[Will you please add your name to stand with me TODAY](https://randpaul.com/f/stand-with-rand?sr=rhf080715nd2)**?

I knew I was walking into a lion's den in last night's debate.

When the Governor of New Jersey attacked me for forcing unconstitutional NSA spying to end, I was ready. If you haven't seen the exchange, you can watch it here.

Fellow Conservative, we fought the American revolution over the right to privacy from government intrusion.

Yet so many in my own party are ready to throw this precious freedom for a false sense of security that is nothing more than a mirage.

As the FBI admitted, the NSA's unconstitutional data collection hasn't stopped one terrorist attack!

But apparently to my opponent from New Jersey, facts don't matter.

Bigger government is always better -- regardless of the cost to American liberty.

Perhaps I shouldn't be surprised.

This is a candidate who stated his goal was to ensure "we don't have an abundance of guns out there."

This is a candidate who treated President Obama as a knight on a white horse as he touched down in New Jersey just before the November 2012 elections -- giving him the campaign photo op he desperately craved.

This is a candidate who's built a massive Big Government record as Governor of New Jersey, and now wants to campaign for President as a conservative!

Fellow Conservative, they all claim to be limited government champions on the campaign trail.

But it's all just hot air.

I'm running for President because our country can't afford another Big Government candidate from EITHER party.

**[Will you stand with me during this critical time](https://randpaul.com/f/stand-with-rand?sr=rhf080715nd2)**?

With the American people now "locking in" like never before to this presidential race, **[will you please chip in $20.16 or give as generously as you can](https://randpaul.com/f/stand-with-rand?sr=rhf080715nd2)**?

Please, Fellow Conservative. This is an absolutely critical time.

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: If Secretary Clinton wants more debates, we'll get them
body:
#
Did you watch last night's Republican presidential debate, friend?
If you are one of the wealthiest people in this country, then you had ten candidates talking about your needs for two hours.
**But in the entire time I watched, I saw very little discussion about the issues important to most American families.** There was no talk about climate change and clean energy, raising wages and providing healthcare for all Americans, criminal justice reform and the undermining of the Voting Rights Act, and nothing at all about the crushing burden of student debt.
And when they did talk about campaign finance reform and the billionaire class buying candidates and elections, it was the butt of a Donald Trump joke.
We need to be discussing issues facing working families at a debate hosted by trade unions. We need to discussing climate change and environmental issues at a forum hosted by the environmental community. We need to be discussing civil rights issues and racial injustice at a forum sponsored by civil rights groups. We need to be discussing gay rights at a forum hosted by the LGBT community. In other words, more discussion, more debate is good for the Democratic Party and good for the American people.
I know, and you know, that the best chance for this country is to discuss the issues that matter. Republicans aren't going to do it, so we need more Democratic debates -- more than the four scheduled by the Democratic National Committee before the Iowa Caucuses.
**And I know that if Secretary Clinton wants more debates, we 'll get them.**
**[Sign my petition and tell Secretary Clinton to encourage the Democratic National Committee to schedule more debates before the Iowa Caucuses and New Hampshire primary in February and to allow important constituencies within the Democratic Party to host their own debates.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb2304/43f17890/5db9c9fb/621d4f56/1606857883/VEsH/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/more-debates?source=em150807su-full")**
Here's what I did hear a lot about last night: I heard a group of ten Republicans on stage longing for a return to the days of George W. Bush. The return to more war and tax breaks for the rich, and less jobs and health insurance for most American families.
Do they remember the two wars George Bush put on the credit card?
I do. Some of us voted no.
Do they remember the 800,000 jobs a month we were hemorrhaging when Bush left office?
I do. Some of us voted against the policies that led us there.
The American people deserve more debates -- debates about how we got to where we are today, and how we move this country forward. And if all the candidates running for the Democratic nomination, especially Secretary Clinton, call for more, then we'll get them.
**[Add your name and encourage Secretary Clinton to call on the Democratic National Committee to schedule more debates before Iowa and New Hampshire, and let's start right away.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb2304/43f17890/5db9c9fb/621d4f56/1606857883/VEsE/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/more-debates?source=em150807su-full")**
We are at a moment of truth. We need to face up to the reality of where we are as a nation, and the best ways to move forward.
Thank you for standing with me.
Bernie Sanders

clean_body: Did you watch last night's Republican presidential debate, friend? If you are one of the wealthiest people in this country, then you had ten candidates talking about your needs for two hours. But in the entire time I watched, I saw very little discussion about the issues important to most American families. There was no talk about climate change and clean energy, raising wages and providing healthcare for all Americans, criminal justice reform and the undermining of the Voting Rights Act, and nothing at all about the crushing burden of student debt. And when they did talk about campaign finance reform and the billionaire class buying candidates and elections, it was the butt of a Donald Trump joke. We need to be discussing issues facing working families at a debate hosted by trade unions. We need to discussing climate change and environmental issues at a forum hosted by the environmental community. We need to be discussing civil rights issues and racial injustice at a forum sponsored by civil rights groups. We need to be discussing gay rights at a forum hosted by the LGBT community. In other words, more discussion, more debate is good for the Democratic Party and good for the American people. I know, and you know, that the best chance for this country is to discuss the issues that matter. Republicans aren't going to do it, so we need more Democratic debates -- more than the four scheduled by the Democratic National Committee before the Iowa Caucuses. And I know that if Secretary Clinton wants more debates, we 'll get them. "" Here's what I did hear a lot about last night: I heard a group of ten Republicans on stage longing for a return to the days of George W. Bush. The return to more war and tax breaks for the rich, and less jobs and health insurance for most American families. Do they remember the two wars George Bush put on the credit card? I do. Some of us voted no. Do they remember the 800,000 jobs a month we were hemorrhaging when Bush left office? I do. Some of us voted against the policies that led us there. The American people deserve more debates -- debates about how we got to where we are today, and how we move this country forward. And if all the candidates running for the Democratic nomination, especially Secretary Clinton, call for more, then we'll get them. "" We are at a moment of truth. We need to face up to the reality of where we are as a nation, and the best ways to move forward. Thank you for standing with me.
